traits: Absent-minded — unaware of immediate surroundings, forgetful, daydreaming
Abusive — inflicts physical or psychological maltreatment
Aimless — devoid of direction or purpose
Anxious — full of mental distress, fear of danger or misfortune
Arrogant — overbearing self-importance, snobbish, rebuffs 'inferiors'
Audacious — recklessly bold in defiance of convention, insolent
Bigmouth — loud-mouthed or gossipy
Bigot — partial to own group, intolerant of difference
Blunt — direct without subtlety, frank, brusque, insensitive
Bold — too forward, takes undue liberties, brazen, cheeky
Callous — hardened to emotion, unfeeling, cold
Childish — lacks maturity, puerile
Cruel — mean without care for consequences or feelings
Cursed — befallen by misfortune, damned, suffers for it
Dependent — cannot function without another's direction
Dishonest — given to fraud, deceitful, crooked, underhanded
Disloyal — unfaithful, traitorous
Dubious — fraught with uncertainty, undecided, doubtful
Egotistical — inflated sense of own importance, boastful, pompous
Envious — painfully desirous of another's advantages, covetous, jealous
Erratic — eccentric, bizarre, deviates from customary conduct
Fanatical — excessive zeal, unreasoning, extravagant notions
Fickle — changeable, unstable in affections, capricious
Fierce — extreme intensity of emotion, inclined to react violently
Finicky — excessively particular, hard to please, fussy
Fixated — strong immature attachment, obsession, fetish, quirk
Flirt — playful romantic/sexual overtures, tease, minx
Gluttonous — excess in food or drink, voracious, insatiable
Gruff — brusque or stern in manner, crusty, surly
Gullible — believes anything, easily deceived or duped
Hard — difficult to deal with, hard-hearted
Hedonistic — devoted to pleasure, especially of the senses
Hoity-toity — flighty, frivolous, giddy, capricious
Humourless — cannot find humour in anything, least of all themselves
Hypocritical — contradicts own stated beliefs and actions
Idealist — guided by ideals over practical considerations, unrealistic
Idiotic — foolish, careless, lacks intelligence or care
Ignorant — lacking knowledge or education on a subject
Immature — emotionally undeveloped, juvenile, childish
Impatient — cannot tolerate delay, restless, intolerant
Impious — lacks reverence for gods and their followers
Impish — naughtily or annoyingly playful
Incompetent — unable to execute tasks of any size
Indecisive — lacks decision and firmness under pressure
Indifferent — lacks interest, calm, casually unconcerned, spiritless
Intolerant — narrow-minded, won't tolerate differing opinion
Judgemental — quick to form moral judgements on appearance/reputation
Klutz — clumsy, a blunderer
Lazy — resistant to work or exertion, idle
Lewd — lascivious, given to lust, obscene in speech or song
Liar — compulsively tells false truths, lies repeatedly
Lustful — driven by lust, preoccupied with desire
Meddlesome — intrusive, interfering, given to meddling
Meek — overly submissive, humble, cowed, little courage
Megalomaniac — delusional fantasies of wealth, power, omnipotence
Naïve — lacks worldly experience, guileless, unsophisticated
Nervous — easily agitated, high-strung, jumpy
Non-violent — abstains from violence
Nosey — pries into others' affairs, snoopy, inquisitive
Obsessive — unhealthy compulsive preoccupation
Oppressor — authority who unjustly keeps others down by force
Overambitious — excessive desire for success or achievement
Overconfident — excessively confident, presumptuous
Overemotional — abnormally emotional, oversensitive
Overprotective — protects too much, coddles
Overzealous — excessive enthusiasm and devotion to a cause
Pacifist — opposes war or violence to resolve disputes
Paranoid — extreme irrational fear or distrust of others
Peevish — fretful, discontent, cantankerous, cranky, ornery
Perfectionist — displeased with anything short of perfect
Pessimist — stresses the negative, takes the gloomiest view
Pest — pesters or annoys, a nuisance, a nag
Phobic — severe fear of one specific thing
Practical — level-headed, efficient, no-nonsense
Predictable — easily read, reactions easily foreseen
Proud — excessive self-esteem, shirks help for pride's sake
Rebellious — defies established authority or tradition, insubordinate
Reckless — heedless, foolhardy, disregards consequences
Remorseless — without remorse, merciless, pitiless, relentless
Rigorous — rigidly accurate, demands strict adherence to rules
Sarcastic — subtle mockery, conveys meaning obliquely
Sceptic — instinctively doubts, questions, disagrees
Seducer — leads others astray from duty or principle
Selfish — concerned chiefly or only with oneself
Self-Martyr — performs suffering to manipulate sympathy
Self-righteous — piously sure of own righteousness, sanctimonious
Senile — declining strength or memory from age or disease
Shallow — lacks intellectual depth, concerned with the obvious
Smart Ass — thinks they know it all, annoying in arguments
Soft-hearted — tender to a fault, can't resist helping, ignores risk
Solemn — deeply earnest, serious, sober
Spineless — lacks courage, cowardly, gutless
Spiteful — malicious ill will, vindictive, vengeful
Spoiled — pampered, no notion of work or money, coddled
Squeamish — excessively fastidious, easily disgusted
Stubborn — perversely unyielding, bull-headed, resolute
Superstitious — irrational belief from ignorance or fear
Tactless — lacks consideration in dealing with others
Temperamental — moody, irritable, volatile, excitable
Theatrical — over-dramatizes, loves centre stage
Timid — shy, quiet, shrinks from confrontation and strangers
Tongue-tied — speechless or confused in expression from shyness
Troublemaker — deliberately or unintentionally stirs up trouble
Unlucky — marked by misfortune, ill-fated, doomed
Unpredictable — chaotic, impossible to foresee their next move
Untrustworthy — not worthy of trust, a backstabber
Vain — unduly high opinion of own appearance, conceited, narcissistic
Weak-willed — lacks willpower, easily swayed
Withdrawn — unfriendly, unsociable, aloof
Zealous — a fanatic
physical: Bad acne all over their face.
Eyes squinted as if looking into a bright light.
Has one more tattoo every time they appear — never addressed by anyone who'd know.
Luscious carbon-black hair past their own height, kept in a long braid, never cut since birth.
A face only a mother could love.
A build that appears both muscular and lanky at once.
Fingers just a little too long — a touch of fae blood, maybe.
Skin on the arms like it's recovering from a nasty chemical burn.
A distinctive accent placing them from somewhere far off.
A large scar across their chin.
A mechanical replacement for a few fingers / a hand / an arm.
Always wearing only the finest clothing.
A patch of discoloured skin around an old scar — poisoned long ago, recovered, but it left its mark.
Always some red stain on their hands. Surely just tomato sauce.
Six fingers on each hand instead of five.
Incredibly beautiful — people do double-takes as they pass.
Hair that changes colour with their mood, after a wild-magic accident.
Dresses like a slob; same clothes for weeks.
Missing several fingers from a farming accident.
Missing a finger from a kitchen accident.
A limp from a spell that backfired.
Never looks anyone in the eyes.
Noticeably crooked teeth; hides their mouth while speaking.
An allergy that makes them sneeze constantly.
Constantly hunched; would gain a foot if they stood straight.
A mermaid tattoo on their arm.
Accident-prone — trips, spills, falls, but never fatally.
A large scar over the right eye; the story of how changes every time.
Always covered in dirt from fields, mines, or mud.
A smile and laugh that always feel genuinely welcoming.
A nose clearly broken numerous times.
Features from both the local population and one many miles away.
Wears an ear trumpet to hear.
Constantly bites their nails from nerves.
Breath visible as if it's always a cold winter's day around them.
Long, sharp nails — the stuff of boogeyman stories told to children.
A lock of hair that always sticks up no matter what.
An eye tattoo on their forehead.
Jagged crystals protruding from their body — shrapnel, or a magical disease.
Resembles a prominent royal family a little too closely.
Looks like they could bench-press a prize cow.
Vitiligo — patches of skin without pigment.
Always has ink stains on their hands.
All teeth replaced with wooden ones.
A false tooth (gold or silver); tongues it to check it's still there.
A worn, tired face with three days' stubble and an artful smear of dirt.
Albino — no pigment in skin, hair, or eyes.
Always chewing some root of unknown properties.
Slightly green with pointy teeth — orc or goblin somewhere back the line.
Stretched, low-hanging ears from heavy earrings.
Hair so long it gets stepped on; always putting it up.
